spec.name: Edge of Darkwood
spec.description: The packed earth track from Millbrook's east gate gives way here to the first real darkness of the Darkwood, the ancient oaks closing overhead in a sudden transition that the locals call the Edge. The road behind is still visible in both directions — Millbrook's stone arch a pale shape in the distance — but ahead the track narrows and the canopy thickens until the sky is almost invisible. The smell changes immediately: cold damp leaf litter, old bark, and something faintly organic beneath. Bird calls are different here than in the open land, and there are other sounds further in — movement in the undergrowth, the occasional crack of a branch — that the open road doesn't produce.
